title: Getting Started with Rust
content:
### Getting Started with Rust

To install Rust, use `rustup`, the official Rust toolchain installer:

```bash
# On Windows, download from https://rustup.rs
# On macOS/Linux:
curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh
```

---

### What rustup Installs

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| rustc | The Rust compiler |
| cargo | Package manager and build tool |
| rustdoc | Documentation generator |
| rustfmt | Code formatter |
| clippy | Linter for catching common mistakes |

---

### Creating Your First Project

```bash
cargo new hello_rust
cd hello_rust
```

This creates a project with:
- `Cargo.toml` - manifest file with metadata and dependencies
- `src/main.rs` - source file with a `main` function

Build and run:

```bash
cargo build      # Compile
cargo run        # Build and run
cargo check      # Check for errors without producing binary
```

> **Key Point:** Always prefer `cargo new` over manually creating files. Cargo sets up the standard project structure and a Git repository automatically.

quiz.question: Which command creates a new Rust project?
